   "id": "83a229e1",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Comparación de Modelos y Optimización de Pipeline — Dataset Titanic\n",
    "\n",
    "**Universidad Católica de Santiago de Guayaquil**  ·  Ciencia de Datos / Machine Learning\n",
    "**Modalidad de entrega:** repositorio de GitHub + informe (PDF / Markdown)\n",
    "\n",
    "---\n",
    "\n",
    "## Distribución del trabajo\n",
    "\n",
    "| Integrante | Tareas realizadas |\n",
    "|---|---|\n",
    "| _[Nombre integrante 1]_ | Carga de datos, feature engineering y justificación de variables (secciones 1–2). |\n",
    "| _[Nombre integrante 2]_ | Pipeline sin *data leakage* y definición de los cuatro modelos (secciones 2–3). |\n",
    "| _[Nombre integrante 3]_ | Validación cruzada, búsqueda de hiperparámetros y evaluación comparativa (secciones 4–5). |\n",
    "| _[Nombre integrante 4]_ | Análisis de overfitting, importancia de variables, gráficos e informe (secciones 6–7 + informe). |\n",
    "\n",
    "> **Cómo ejecutar:** abrir el notebook y usar **Kernel → Restart & Run All**. El archivo `titanic.csv` está incluido en el repositorio, por lo que el notebook corre **sin conexión a internet**. Dependencias: `pandas`, `numpy`, `scikit-learn`, `matplotlib`.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "9a9a99c6",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Objetivo del trabajo\n",
    "\n",
    "A partir del clasificador básico visto en clases, este trabajo construye un **pipeline de Machine Learning reproducible y sin *data leakage*** para predecir la supervivencia de los pasajeros del Titanic. Vamos más allá de lo visto en clases en cuatro frentes:\n",
    "\n",
    "1. **Feature engineering propio:** variables nuevas justificadas (tarifa por persona, viaje en solitario, interacción clase–edad, cubierta).\n",
    "2. **Comparación rigurosa de cuatro modelos** bajo idénticas condiciones.\n",
    "3. **Búsqueda sistemática de hiperparámetros** con validación cruzada (`GridSearchCV`, `cv=5`).\n",
    "4. **Evaluación crítica:** métricas más allá del *accuracy*, análisis de overfitting e importancia de variables.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "4eef8315",

    "from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline\n",
    "from sklearn.compose import ColumnTransformer\n",
    "from sklearn.impute import SimpleImputer\n",

    "from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier\n",
    "from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier, GradientBoostingClassifier\n",
    "from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression\n",

    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# Carga del dataset Titanic (formato Kaggle: incluye Name y Cabin)\n",
    "# ------------------------------------------------------------\n",
    "# Se lee el archivo local 'titanic.csv' incluido en el repositorio\n",
    "# para que el notebook sea 100% reproducible sin internet. Si no\n",
    "# estuviera, se descarga de una fuente publica estable.\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "CSV_LOCAL = \"titanic.csv\"\n",
    "CSV_URL = \"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datasciencedojo/datasets/master/titanic.csv\"\n",
    "\n",
    "try:\n",
    "    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_LOCAL)\n",
    "    print(f\"Datos cargados desde archivo local: {CSV_LOCAL}\")\n",
    "except FileNotFoundError:\n",
    "    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_URL)\n",
    "    print(\"Archivo local no encontrado; datos descargados de la fuente publica.\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Dimensiones: {df.shape[0]} filas x {df.shape[1]} columnas\")\n",
    "display(df.head())\n",
    "\n",
    "balance = df[\"Survived\"].value_counts(normalize=True).rename(\n",
    "    {0: \"No sobrevivio\", 1: \"Sobrevivio\"})\n",
    "print(\"\\nDistribucion de la clase objetivo (Survived):\")\n",
    "print((balance * 100).round(1).astype(str) + \" %\")\n",
    "nulos = df.isnull().sum()\n",
    "print(\"\\nValores faltantes por columna:\")\n",
    "print(nulos[nulos > 0])\n"
   ]
  },

   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "498ef762",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. Preprocesamiento y Feature Engineering\n",
    "\n",
    "Partimos del feature engineering visto en clases (extracción del **título** del nombre, **tamaño del grupo familiar** e **indicador de cabina**) y **agregamos variables nuevas**. Cada una se justifica a continuación:\n",
    "\n",
    "- **`FarePerPerson`** = `Fare / FamilySize`. La tarifa registrada corresponde al billete completo del grupo, no a cada persona. Dividirla por el tamaño del grupo aísla el verdadero nivel socioeconómico individual, ligado al acceso a cubiertas y botes salvavidas.\n",
    "- **`IsAlone`** (1 si `FamilySize == 1`, 0 si no). Viajar solo o acompañado cambia la dinámica de evacuación: las familias se buscaban entre sí y los niños viajaban acompañados. Resume de forma no lineal el tamaño familiar.\n",
    "- **`Pclass_x_Age`** = `Pclass × Age`. Término de **interacción**: el efecto de la edad sobre la supervivencia depende de la clase (un niño de primera clase no tenía las mismas probabilidades que uno de tercera). Los modelos lineales no lo infieren por sí solos.\n",
    "- **`Deck`** = primera letra del número de cabina (faltante → `M`). Indica la ubicación física en el barco y la cercanía a los botes; las cubiertas superiores correspondían a clases altas.\n",
    "- **`Title`** (Mr, Mrs, Miss, Master, Rare) y **`FamilySize`** provienen de lo visto en clases y se conservan por su alto poder predictivo (el título resume sexo, edad aproximada y estatus social).\n",
    "\n",
    "El feature engineering se implementa como un transformador **fila a fila y sin estado** (`TitanicFeatureEngineer`): cada variable nueva depende solo de los datos de ese pasajero, por lo que **no introduce *data leakage*** y puede ser el primer paso del Pipeline.\n"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# 1. Feature Engineering - Transformador personalizado (sin estado)\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "class TitanicFeatureEngineer(BaseEstimator, TransformerMixin):\n",
    "    \"\"\"Crea variables derivadas a partir de las columnas crudas del Titanic.\n",
    "\n",
    "    Es fila a fila y sin estado (fit no aprende nada), por lo que NO\n",
    "    produce data leakage y puede ir como primer paso del Pipeline.\n",
    "    \"\"\"\n",
    "\n",
    "    TITLE_MAP = {\n",
    "        \"Mr\": \"Mr\", \"Mrs\": \"Mrs\", \"Miss\": \"Miss\", \"Master\": \"Master\",\n",
    "        \"Mme\": \"Mrs\", \"Ms\": \"Miss\", \"Mlle\": \"Miss\",\n",
    "        \"Dr\": \"Rare\", \"Rev\": \"Rare\", \"Col\": \"Rare\", \"Major\": \"Rare\",\n",
    "        \"Capt\": \"Rare\", \"Sir\": \"Rare\", \"Lady\": \"Rare\", \"Don\": \"Rare\",\n",
    "        \"Dona\": \"Rare\", \"Countess\": \"Rare\", \"Jonkheer\": \"Rare\",\n",
    "    }\n",
    "    VALID_DECKS = set(\"ABCDEFG\")\n",
    "\n",
    "    def fit(self, X, y=None):\n",
    "        return self\n",
    "\n",
    "    def transform(self, X):\n",
    "        X = X.copy()\n",
    "\n",
    "        # (a) Titulo social extraido del nombre\n",
    "        X[\"Title\"] = (X[\"Name\"].str.extract(r\",\\s*([^\\.]+)\\.\", expand=False)\n",
    "                                 .str.strip()\n",
    "                                 .map(self.TITLE_MAP)\n",
    "                                 .fillna(\"Rare\"))\n",
    "\n",
    "        # (b) Tamano del grupo familiar a bordo\n",
    "        X[\"FamilySize\"] = X[\"SibSp\"] + X[\"Parch\"] + 1\n",
    "\n",
    "        # (c) Viajaba solo?  (1 = si, 0 = no)\n",
    "        X[\"IsAlone\"] = (X[\"FamilySize\"] == 1).astype(int)\n",
    "\n",
    "        # (d) Tarifa por persona\n",
    "        X[\"FarePerPerson\"] = X[\"Fare\"] / X[\"FamilySize\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "        # (e) Cubierta (primera letra de la cabina); faltante -> 'M'\n",
    "        deck = X[\"Cabin\"].astype(str).str[0]\n",
    "        X[\"Deck\"] = deck.where(deck.isin(self.VALID_DECKS), other=\"M\")\n",
    "\n",
    "        # (f) Interaccion Pclass * Age\n",
    "        X[\"Pclass_x_Age\"] = X[\"Pclass\"] * X[\"Age\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "        return X.drop(columns=[\"PassengerId\", \"Name\", \"Ticket\", \"Cabin\"],\n",
    "                      errors=\"ignore\")\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "# Vista rapida del resultado del feature engineering\n",
    "_demo = TitanicFeatureEngineer().fit_transform(df.drop(columns=\"Survived\"))\n",
    "print(\"Columnas tras el feature engineering:\")\n",
    "print(list(_demo.columns))\n",
    "display(_demo[[\"Title\", \"FamilySize\", \"IsAlone\", \"FarePerPerson\",\n",
    "               \"Deck\", \"Pclass_x_Age\"]].head())\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "84670ec5",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Pipeline sin *data leakage*\n",
    "\n",
    "La imputación de faltantes, el escalado y la codificación **sí aprenden parámetros de los datos** (la mediana, la media/desviación, las categorías). Para evitar fuga de información se encapsulan en un `ColumnTransformer` dentro de un `Pipeline`: en cada *fold* de la validación cruzada estos pasos se ajustan **solo con los datos de entrenamiento** de ese fold y luego se aplican al de validación. No existe ninguna transformación manual sobre el dataset completo.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "751543cf",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.677178Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.677017Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.682588Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.681749Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Variables numericas : ['Pclass', 'Age', 'SibSp', 'Parch', 'Fare', 'FamilySize', 'IsAlone', 'FarePerPerson', 'Pclass_x_Age']\n",
      "Variables categoricas: ['Sex', 'Embarked', 'Title', 'Deck']\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# 2. Preprocesamiento encapsulado (ColumnTransformer)\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "numeric_features = [\"Pclass\", \"Age\", \"SibSp\", \"Parch\", \"Fare\",\n",
    "                    \"FamilySize\", \"IsAlone\", \"FarePerPerson\", \"Pclass_x_Age\"]\n",
    "categorical_features = [\"Sex\", \"Embarked\", \"Title\", \"Deck\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "def make_preprocessor():\n",
    "    \"\"\"Devuelve un ColumnTransformer nuevo (evita estado compartido).\"\"\"\n",
    "    numeric_pipe = Pipeline([\n",
    "        (\"imputer\", SimpleImputer(strategy=\"median\")),\n",
    "        (\"scaler\", StandardScaler()),\n",
    "    ])\n",
    "    categorical_pipe = Pipeline([\n",
    "        (\"imputer\", SimpleImputer(strategy=\"most_frequent\")),\n",
    "        (\"onehot\", OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown=\"ignore\", sparse_output=False)),\n",
    "    ])\n",
    "    return ColumnTransformer([\n",
    "        (\"num\", numeric_pipe, numeric_features),\n",
    "        (\"cat\", categorical_pipe, categorical_features),\n",
    "    ], remainder=\"drop\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Variables numericas :\", numeric_features)\n",
    "print(\"Variables categoricas:\", categorical_features)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "be70cf85",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### Separación entrenamiento / prueba\n",
    "\n",
    "Se reserva el **20 %** de los datos como conjunto de prueba, **estratificado** para conservar la proporción de sobrevivientes. El conjunto de prueba **no se usa** durante el entrenamiento ni en la búsqueda de hiperparámetros.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 5,
   "id": "281860a8",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.684738Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.684045Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.692856Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.692016Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Entrenamiento: 712 pasajeros\n",
      "Prueba:        179 pasajeros\n",
      "Proporcion de sobrevivientes -> train: 0.383 | test: 0.385\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# Separacion entrenamiento / prueba (estratificada 80/20)\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "X = df.drop(columns=\"Survived\")\n",
    "y = df[\"Survived\"]\n",
    "\n",
    "X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(\n",
    "    X, y, test_size=0.20, stratify=y, random_state=RANDOM_STATE)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(f\"Entrenamiento: {X_train.shape[0]} pasajeros\")\n",
    "print(f\"Prueba:        {X_test.shape[0]} pasajeros\")\n",
    "print(f\"Proporcion de sobrevivientes -> train: {y_train.mean():.3f} | \"\n",
    "      f\"test: {y_test.mean():.3f}\")\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a89dd05c",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Modelos a comparar\n",
    "\n",
    "Se entrenan y comparan cuatro modelos con el **mismo preprocesamiento**:\n",
    "\n",
    "1. **Árbol de Decisión** (`DecisionTreeClassifier`) — modelo base interpretable.\n",
    "2. **Random Forest** (`RandomForestClassifier`) — ensamble por *bagging*.\n",
    "3. **Gradient Boosting** (`GradientBoostingClassifier`) — ensamble por *boosting*.\n",
    "4. **Regresión Logística** (`LogisticRegression`) — *cuarto modelo elegido por el grupo*.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Justificación del cuarto modelo (Regresión Logística):** es una línea base lineal, rápida y altamente interpretable (sus coeficientes son *log-odds*). Sirve de referencia para juzgar si la complejidad adicional de los ensambles realmente aporta capacidad predictiva o si un modelo simple basta. Se usa `GradientBoostingClassifier` de scikit-learn en lugar de XGBoost para mantener el entorno reproducible sin dependencias externas.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 6,
   "id": "55de16de",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.694464Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.694322Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.699937Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.699048Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Modelos a comparar:\n",
      "  - Arbol de Decision\n",
      "  - Random Forest\n",
      "  - Gradient Boosting\n",
      "  - Regresion Logistica\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# 3. Definicion de los cuatro modelos (mismo preprocesamiento)\n",
    "# ------------------------------------------------------------\n",
    "# Cada modelo es un Pipeline completo:\n",
    "#   FeatureEngineering -> preprocesador -> clasificador\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "def build_pipeline(classifier):\n",
    "    return Pipeline([\n",
    "        (\"features\", TitanicFeatureEngineer()),\n",
    "        (\"preprocessor\", make_preprocessor()),\n",
    "        (\"classifier\", classifier),\n",
    "    ])\n",
    "\n",
    "models = {\n",
    "    \"Arbol de Decision\":   build_pipeline(DecisionTreeClassifier(random_state=RANDOM_STATE)),\n",
    "    \"Random Forest\":       build_pipeline(RandomForestClassifier(random_state=RANDOM_STATE, n_jobs=-1)),\n",
    "    \"Gradient Boosting\":   build_pipeline(GradientBoostingClassifier(random_state=RANDOM_STATE)),\n",
    "    \"Regresion Logistica\": build_pipeline(LogisticRegression(max_iter=2000, random_state=RANDOM_STATE)),\n",
    "}\n",
    "print(\"Modelos a comparar:\")\n",
    "for name in models:\n",
    "    print(\"  -\", name)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "a700d4d0",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "### Diagnóstico inicial de overfitting\n",
    "\n",
    "Antes de afinar, comparamos el *accuracy* de entrenamiento con el de validación cruzada usando los **parámetros por defecto**, para identificar qué modelos tienden a sobreajustar.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 7,
   "id": "3e08df12",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.701928Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-23T00:20:13.701179Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-23T00:20:16.146420Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-23T00:20:16.145376Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "text/html": [
       "<div>\n",

      ],
      "text/plain": [
       "                Modelo  Acc. entrenamiento  Acc. validacion (CV)  \\\n",
       "0    Arbol de Decision               0.987                 0.788   \n",
       "1        Random Forest               0.987                 0.812   \n",
       "2    Gradient Boosting               0.927                 0.820   \n",

       "\n",

      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# Diagnostico inicial de overfitting (parametros por defecto)\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "baseline_rows = []\n",
    "for name, pipe in models.items():\n",
    "    pipe.fit(X_train, y_train)\n",
    "    train_acc = pipe.score(X_train, y_train)\n",
    "    cv_acc = cross_val_score(pipe, X_train, y_train, cv=cv, scoring=\"accuracy\").mean()\n",
    "    baseline_rows.append({\n",
    "        \"Modelo\": name,\n",
    "        \"Acc. entrenamiento\": round(train_acc, 3),\n",
    "        \"Acc. validacion (CV)\": round(cv_acc, 3),\n",
    "        \"Brecha (train - CV)\": round(train_acc - cv_acc, 3),\n",
    "    })\n",
    "\n",
    "baseline_df = pd.DataFrame(baseline_rows)\n",
    "display(baseline_df)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "3e2a02b1",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 4. Validación cruzada y búsqueda de hiperparámetros\n",
    "\n",
    "Se aplica `GridSearchCV` con **`cv=5`** (validación cruzada estratificada). Aunque la consigna exige afinar al menos dos modelos, **afinamos los cuatro** para que la comparación sea justa. Para cada modelo se reportan los hiperparámetros explorados, los mejores encontrados y la métrica de selección.\n",
    "\n",
    "**Métrica de selección (`scoring='roc_auc'`) — justificación:** la clase está desbalanceada (~62 % no sobrevivió / ~38 % sobrevivió), por lo que el *accuracy* premia acertar la clase mayoritaria y puede ocultar un mal desempeño en la minoritaria. El **AUC-ROC** mide la capacidad del modelo de **ordenar** correctamente a los pasajeros por probabilidad de supervivencia, es independiente del umbral de decisión y robusto al desbalance; por eso lo preferimos frente al *accuracy* como criterio de selección.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 8,
   "id": "54f870d3",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-23T00:20:16.148926Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-23T00:20:16.148216Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-23T00:21:31.666302Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-23T00:21:31.665245Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "> Arbol de Decision\n",
      "    Combinaciones probadas : 24\n",
      "    Mejor AUC (CV 5-fold)  : 0.8714\n",
      "    Mejores hiperparametros: {'criterion': 'entropy', 'max_depth': 3, 'min_samples_leaf': 10}\n",
      "\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "> Random Forest\n",
      "    Combinaciones probadas : 24\n",
      "    Mejor AUC (CV 5-fold)  : 0.8874\n",
      "    Mejores hiperparametros: {'max_depth': None, 'max_features': 'sqrt', 'min_samples_leaf': 3, 'n_estimators': 400}\n",
      "\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "> Gradient Boosting\n",
      "    Combinaciones probadas : 16\n",
      "    Mejor AUC (CV 5-fold)  : 0.8951\n",
      "    Mejores hiperparametros: {'learning_rate': 0.05, 'max_depth': 3, 'n_estimators': 200, 'subsample': 0.8}\n",
      "\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "> Regresion Logistica\n",
      "    Combinaciones probadas : 4\n",
      "    Mejor AUC (CV 5-fold)  : 0.8700\n",
      "    Mejores hiperparametros: {'C': 1, 'penalty': 'l2'}\n",
      "\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# 4. Busqueda de hiperparametros con GridSearchCV (cv=5, scoring=roc_auc)\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "param_grids = {\n",
    "    \"Arbol de Decision\": {\n",
    "        \"classifier__max_depth\": [3, 5, 7, None],\n",
    "        \"classifier__min_samples_leaf\": [1, 5, 10],\n",
    "        \"classifier__criterion\": [\"gini\", \"entropy\"],\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    \"Random Forest\": {\n",
    "        \"classifier__n_estimators\": [200, 400],\n",
    "        \"classifier__max_depth\": [5, 10, None],\n",
    "        \"classifier__min_samples_leaf\": [1, 3],\n",
    "        \"classifier__max_features\": [\"sqrt\", 0.5],\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    \"Gradient Boosting\": {\n",
    "        \"classifier__n_estimators\": [100, 200],\n",
    "        \"classifier__learning_rate\": [0.05, 0.1],\n",
    "        \"classifier__max_depth\": [2, 3],\n",
    "        \"classifier__subsample\": [0.8, 1.0],\n",
    "    },\n",
    "    \"Regresion Logistica\": {\n",
    "        \"classifier__C\": [0.01, 0.1, 1, 10],\n",
    "        \"classifier__penalty\": [\"l2\"],\n",
    "    },\n",
    "}\n",
    "\n",
    "best_models, search_summary = {}, {}\n",
    "for name, pipe in models.items():\n",
    "    grid = GridSearchCV(pipe, param_grids[name], scoring=\"roc_auc\",\n",
    "                        cv=cv, n_jobs=-1, refit=True)\n",
    "    grid.fit(X_train, y_train)\n",
    "    best_models[name] = grid.best_estimator_\n",
    "    search_summary[name] = {\n",
    "        \"mejor_auc_cv\": round(grid.best_score_, 4),\n",
    "        \"mejores_parametros\": {k.replace(\"classifier__\", \"\"): v\n",
    "                               for k, v in grid.best_params_.items()},\n",
    "        \"n_combinaciones\": len(grid.cv_results_[\"params\"]),\n",
    "    }\n",
    "    print(f\"> {name}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"    Combinaciones probadas : {search_summary[name]['n_combinaciones']}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"    Mejor AUC (CV 5-fold)  : {grid.best_score_:.4f}\")\n",
    "    print(f\"    Mejores hiperparametros: {search_summary[name]['mejores_parametros']}\")\n",
    "    print()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "4d7f54d7",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 5. Evaluación comparativa\n",
    "\n",
    "Sobre el **conjunto de prueba** se reportan, para cada modelo con sus mejores hiperparámetros: *accuracy*, *precision* (macro), *recall* (macro), *F1-score* (macro) y *AUC-ROC*. Se incluye además la **matriz de confusión del mejor modelo** y la **curva ROC de los cuatro modelos** en un mismo gráfico con sus AUC en la leyenda.\n"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# 5. Tabla comparativa sobre el conjunto de PRUEBA\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "def evaluate_on_test(name, model):\n",
    "    y_pred = model.predict(X_test)\n",
    "    y_proba = model.predict_proba(X_test)[:, 1]\n",
    "    return {\n",
    "        \"Modelo\": name,\n",
    "        \"Accuracy\": accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred),\n",
    "        \"Precision (macro)\": precision_score(y_test, y_pred, average=\"macro\"),\n",
    "        \"Recall (macro)\": recall_score(y_test, y_pred, average=\"macro\"),\n",
    "        \"F1-score (macro)\": f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average=\"macro\"),\n",
    "        \"AUC-ROC\": roc_auc_score(y_test, y_proba),\n",
    "    }\n",
    "\n",
    "comparison_df = (pd.DataFrame([evaluate_on_test(n, m) for n, m in best_models.items()])\n",
    "                 .set_index(\"Modelo\").round(4))\n",
    "display(comparison_df.sort_values(\"AUC-ROC\", ascending=False)\n",
    "        .style.background_gradient(cmap=\"Greens\", axis=0)\n",
    "        .format(\"{:.4f}\"))\n"
   ]
  },

    "# ------------------------------------------------------------\n",
    "# Mejor modelo segun AUC-ROC en prueba  +  matriz de confusion\n",
    "# ------------------------------------------------------------\n",
    "best_name = comparison_df[\"AUC-ROC\"].idxmax()\n",
    "best_model = best_models[best_name]\n",
    "print(f\"Mejor modelo: {best_name}  \"\n",
    "      f\"(AUC-ROC = {comparison_df.loc[best_name, 'AUC-ROC']:.4f})\\n\")\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(5, 4.2))\n",
    "ConfusionMatrixDisplay.from_estimator(\n",
    "    best_model, X_test, y_test,\n",
    "    display_labels=[\"No sobrevivio\", \"Sobrevivio\"],\n",
    "    cmap=\"Blues\", colorbar=False, ax=ax)\n",
    "ax.set_title(f\"Matriz de confusion - {best_name}\")\n",
    "ax.grid(False)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.savefig(FIG_DIR / \"matriz_confusion.png\", bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"Reporte de clasificacion del mejor modelo:\\n\")\n",
    "print(classification_report(y_test, best_model.predict(X_test),\n",
    "                            target_names=[\"No sobrevivio\", \"Sobrevivio\"]))\n"
   ]

    "## 6. Análisis de overfitting\n",
    "\n",

   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# 6. Analisis de overfitting (modelos ya afinados)\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "over_rows = []\n",
    "for name, model in best_models.items():\n",
    "    train_acc = model.score(X_train, y_train)\n",
    "    cv_scores = cross_val_score(model, X_train, y_train, cv=cv, scoring=\"accuracy\")\n",
    "    over_rows.append({\n",
    "        \"Modelo\": name,\n",
    "        \"Acc. entrenamiento\": train_acc,\n",
    "        \"Acc. CV (media)\": cv_scores.mean(),\n",
    "        \"Desv. CV\": cv_scores.std(),\n",
    "        \"Brecha (train - CV)\": train_acc - cv_scores.mean(),\n",
    "    })\n",
    "overfit_df = pd.DataFrame(over_rows).set_index(\"Modelo\").round(3)\n",
    "display(overfit_df)\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.5, 4.5))\n",
    "xpos = np.arange(len(overfit_df)); w = 0.38\n",
    "ax.bar(xpos - w/2, overfit_df[\"Acc. entrenamiento\"], w,\n",
    "       label=\"Entrenamiento\", color=\"#3b6fb5\")\n",
    "ax.bar(xpos + w/2, overfit_df[\"Acc. CV (media)\"], w,\n",
    "       label=\"Validacion cruzada\", color=\"#e08a3c\")\n",
    "ax.set_xticks(xpos)\n",
    "ax.set_xticklabels(overfit_df.index, rotation=15, ha=\"right\")\n",
    "ax.set_ylabel(\"Accuracy\"); ax.set_ylim(0.7, 1.0)\n",
    "ax.set_title(\"Entrenamiento vs. validacion cruzada (deteccion de overfitting)\")\n",
    "ax.legend()\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.savefig(FIG_DIR / \"overfitting.png\", bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },

   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# 7. Importancia de variables (mejor modelo)\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "clf = best_model.named_steps[\"classifier\"]\n",
    "prep = best_model.named_steps[\"preprocessor\"]\n",
    "feat_eng = best_model.named_steps[\"features\"]\n",
    "feat_names = [n.replace(\"num__\", \"\").replace(\"cat__\", \"\")\n",
    "              for n in prep.get_feature_names_out()]\n",
    "\n",
    "if hasattr(clf, \"feature_importances_\"):\n",
    "    # Modelos de arbol: importancia por reduccion de impureza\n",
    "    importances = clf.feature_importances_\n",
    "    metodo = \"feature_importances_ (impureza)\"\n",
    "elif hasattr(clf, \"coef_\"):\n",
    "    # Modelo lineal: como las variables estan estandarizadas, la magnitud\n",
    "    # del coeficiente es directamente comparable entre variables.\n",
    "    importances = np.abs(clf.coef_).ravel()\n",
    "    metodo = \"|coeficiente estandarizado|\"\n",
    "else:\n",
    "    # Alternativa general: importancia por permutacion sobre la matriz transformada\n",
    "    X_test_t = prep.transform(feat_eng.transform(X_test))\n",
    "    r = permutation_importance(clf, X_test_t, y_test, scoring=\"roc_auc\",\n",
    "                               n_repeats=15, random_state=RANDOM_STATE, n_jobs=-1)\n",
    "    importances = r.importances_mean\n",
    "    metodo = \"permutation_importance\"\n",
    "\n",
    "imp_df = (pd.DataFrame({\"Variable\": feat_names, \"Importancia\": importances})\n",
    "          .sort_values(\"Importancia\", ascending=False).head(10).reset_index(drop=True))\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.5, 5))\n",
    "ax.barh(imp_df[\"Variable\"][::-1], imp_df[\"Importancia\"][::-1], color=\"#3b6fb5\")\n",
    "ax.set_title(f\"Top 10 variables mas importantes - {best_name}\")\n",
    "ax.set_xlabel(f\"Importancia ({metodo})\")\n",
    "ax.grid(axis=\"y\", alpha=0)\n",
    "plt.tight_layout()\n",
    "plt.savefig(FIG_DIR / \"importancia_variables.png\", bbox_inches=\"tight\")\n",
    "plt.show()\n",
    "\n",
    "display(imp_df)\n"
   ]
  },
  {

   "source": [
    "### Exportación de resultados\n",
    "\n",
    "Se guardan los números clave en `resultados.json` (alimentan el informe en PDF/Markdown).\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 14,
   "id": "1d4308f1",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-06-23T00:21:37.469224Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-06-23T00:21:37.469066Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-06-23T00:21:37.489039Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-06-23T00:21:37.488177Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "Resultados guardados en resultados.json\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "# Exportar resultados clave para el informe\n",
    "# ============================================================\n",
    "import json\n",
    "\n",
    "cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, best_model.predict(X_test))\n",
    "resultados = {\n",
    "    \"n_total\": int(df.shape[0]),\n",
    "    \"n_train\": int(X_train.shape[0]),\n",
    "    \"n_test\": int(X_test.shape[0]),\n",
    "    \"balance_pct\": {\"No sobrevivio\": round(float((y == 0).mean()) * 100, 1),\n",
    "                    \"Sobrevivio\": round(float((y == 1).mean()) * 100, 1)},\n",
    "    \"tabla_comparativa\": comparison_df.reset_index().round(4).to_dict(orient=\"records\"),\n",
    "    \"mejor_modelo\": best_name,\n",
    "    \"busqueda\": search_summary,\n",
    "    \"overfitting\": overfit_df.reset_index().round(3).to_dict(orient=\"records\"),\n",
    "    \"baseline_overfitting\": baseline_df.to_dict(orient=\"records\"),\n",
    "    \"top_features\": imp_df.round(4).to_dict(orient=\"records\"),\n",
    "    \"matriz_confusion\": {\"tn\": int(cm[0, 0]), \"fp\": int(cm[0, 1]),\n",
    "                         \"fn\": int(cm[1, 0]), \"tp\": int(cm[1, 1])},\n",
    "}\n",
    "with open(\"resultados.json\", \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n",
    "    json.dump(resultados, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)\n",
    "print(\"Resultados guardados en resultados.json\")\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",

   "source": [
    "## Conclusiones\n",
    "\n",
    "El análisis completo (recomendación de modelo, limitaciones y próximos pasos) se desarrolla en el **informe** adjunto (`informe.pdf` / `informe.md`). En síntesis, el trabajo prioriza **entender por qué un modelo supera a otro** y aplicar buenas prácticas de ML por encima de maximizar el AUC.\n"
